L1.area: Sandbox filesystem isolation and container path resolution layer

L2.description: A TOCTOU race condition exists in the sandbox filesystem isolation layer where staged file operations validate the destination path before committing, but the temporary file creation and population are not anchored to the validated canonical parent directory. An attacker can race a parent-path alias change between the validation step and the actual file materialization, causing content to be written outside the intended writable mount boundary.

L3.area: Sandbox filesystem bridge write staging and stat command plan construction
L3.description: A TOCTOU race condition exists in the sandbox filesystem bridge where file write operations resolve and validate the target path but do not anchor the temporary file creation to the canonical parent directory. When a parent path contains a symlink alias, an attacker can change the symlink target between the path validation step and the actual staged write, causing the temporary file to be created outside the allowed writable mount before the final guarded replace step runs. The stat operation is similarly affected by not decomposing targets into a canonical parent and basename before execution.

ground_truth.reason: The sandbox fs-bridge writeFile and stat operations validated the destination path but created temporary files using shell path operations that were not pinned to the canonical parent directory, allowing a TOCTOU race via symlink alias changes to write outside the intended writable mount.
